data.title: Delta soars to 52-week high despite operational issues in snowy Atlanta
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) is having a strong day on Wall Street, with shares up more than 9%. However, the airline company has had some operational headaches in Atlanta, where a winter storm has forced the closure of five runways.

The carrier also had to suspend the takeoff of flight 2668 from Atlanta to Minneapolis-St. Paul after an indication of an engine issue. Four passengers were reported to have had minor injuries.

Investors looked past the issues in Atlanta after Delta Air Lines' (NYSE:DAL) 2025 guidance topped expectations amid strong demand trends over the last six weeks. CEO Ed Bastian said he expects the full year could be the company's best in its history. "We feel quite good coming into the new year," Bastian said in a media interview. "Everywhere, we see consumers continue to prioritize experience over goods," he added.

Shares of Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) traded at a new 52-week high of $68.98 earlier in the session. The airline stock was up 9.45% at 1:00 p.m. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on Delta (DAL) has been flashing Buy since October.

data.title: Apple's foldable iPhone might face shipping challenges: TF
data.description:
Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) foldable iPhone, which is currently in the planning stage, is only expected to support eSIM due to its ultra-thin design, which could result in shipping challenges, according to TF Internation Securities analysts Ming-Chi Kuo.

"Given that the Chinese market currently does not promote phones that only support eSIM, these two models could face shipping momentum challenges unless their design is modified," Kuo said in a post today on Medium.

"An eSIM is an industry-standard digital SIM that allows you to activate a cellular plan from your carrier without having to use a physical SIM," according to Apple. "You can install eight or more eSIMs on an iPhone and use two phone numbers at the same time."

Apple points out that the eSIM on an iPhone is not offered in mainland China. Instead, Dual SIM is required, which is two nano-SIM cards.

"Although the ultra-thin iPhone 17 may ship in higher volumes than the iPhone Plus, it likely won’t significantly boost iPhone sales overall, partly due to downgraded components paired with a high price and a user experience similar to current models," Kuo said.

Overall, Kuo forecasts that total iPhone shipments might fall 8% to 10% less than the consensus.

"Even with the new iPhone SE4 (expected to launch around mid-1H25), iPhone 1H25 shipments are projected to decline by about 6% YoY," Kuo added.

Barclays analysts echoed this sentiment in a research note on Thursday, saying that 2025 will start out "mixed at best" when Apple reports its all-important fiscal first-quarter results on Jan. 30. After the earnings release, the analysts said they believe there will be "some" iPhone recovery, as the company's installed base gets older, but numbers will be "choppy" throughout 2025.

Apple shares slid about 3% during afternoon trading on Friday.

data.title: Citi anticipates further global growth in 2025
data.description:
The Citi Research team indicated on Friday morning that they expect a strong and positive year for global growth, citing favorable economic conditions and ongoing market trends.

"Global equities rose in 2024, but gains were concentrated in the US and its tech giants. In 2025, we expect equities to rise once again, forecasting 10% upside for the MSCI AC World," Citi Research said in an investor note.

While the financial firm is optimistic about global growth, it highlighted that it continues to maintain an overweight position in both the U.S. and continental Europe.

Citi went on to highliht that it remains balanced in its global portfolio stance. From a sector-by-sector vantage point, it spotlighted that it upgraded Health Care to overweight, joining Communication Services and Financials. At the same time it downgraded Consumer Discrtionary, Utilities, and Industrials to underweight.

As for what lies ahead for global growth, Citi Research outlined three key themes: "Navigating Trump 2.0, broadening EPS [along with] broadening markets,[and] rates debate."

For investors looking to keep a pulse on the global market, outlined below are a handful of exchange traded funds that provide global exposure.

Global ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VTI), (NYSEARCA:SPGM), (QWLD), (URTH), (VT), (NYSEARCA:IOO), (DFAI), (IDV), (VGK), (EZU), (BBEU), (FEZ), (IEV),

data.title: Bayer MRI contrast agent gadoquatrane meets goals in phase 3 trials
data.description:
• Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) said that phase 3 data on its experimental MRI contrast agent gadoquatrane met primary and secondary endpoints in phase 3 trials.
  • The QUANTI studies examined gadoquatrane at a gadolinium dose of 0.04 mmol Gd/kg body weight, a gadolinium dose reduction of 60% compared to macrocyclic gadolinium-based contrast agents dosed at 0.1 mmol Gd/kg body weight.
  • Diagnostic efficacy endpoints were met with the lower gadoquatrane dose. Gadoquatrane met the primary and main secondary efficacy endpoints assessing visualization parameters and lesion detection.

data.title: Oil is the most oversold asset globally – BofA
data.description:
Oil (USO) is the most oversold asset around the world, with flows down 5% from their 200-day moving average, BofA Securities data showed.

Oil also had the highest ranked returns in 2024, up 2.2%, and hedge fund positioning for oil futures (CL1:COM) has turned bullish, according to BofA Bull & Bear Indicator.

On the other hand, U.S. equities (SP500) is the most overbought asset.

data.title: BofA sees no fed rate cuts in 2025 after ‘gangbusters’ Dec jobs report
data.description:
Bank of America said Friday it no longer expects the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates in 2025 following the stronger-than-expected December jobs report.

Risks appeared skewed toward monetary policymakers shifting to raising interest rates, BofA said in a note. “The Dec jobs report was gangbusters,” Aditya Bhave, BofA Global Research's senior U.S. economist said. “Given a resilient labor market, we now think the Fed cutting cycle is over,” he said.

The last payrolls report of 2024 also prompted Goldman Sachs to say it now sees only two rate reductions in 2025. Nonfarm payrolls surged by 256K versus +157K consensus estimate. The unemployment rate fell to 4.1% vs. expectations of a 4.2% reading.

“Our base case has the Fed on an extended hold,” with inflation stuck above the Fed’s 2% target and policymakers last month lifting their 2025 inflation view, Bhave said. “But we think the risks for the next move are skewed toward a hike” if core PCE inflation Y/Y exceeds 3% and/or long-term inflation expectations become unanchored, the economist said.

Traders on Friday were pricing in expectations for a quarter-point rate cut in September, pushing back an anticipated June start. The Federal Open Market Committee slashed rates by 100 basis points in 2024, to a 4.25%-4.5% range.

Separately, Goldman Sachs now expects two quarter-point rate reductions this year, down from its previous call of three cuts. It sees cuts coming in June and December, followed by another snip in June 2026, to a terminal rate of 3.5%-3.75%.

BofA’s Bhave said markets were still pricing 30-35bp of cuts in 2025. “We see this mostly as risk premium, in case the economy weakens substantially,” he said.

Stocks (SP500)(NYSEARCA:SPY)(COMP:IND)(DJI) sharply dropped after the jobs report, with the Nasdaq Composite falling more than 2% during Friday’s session.

Treasury yields (US10Y)(US2Y) jumped, pushing the 10-year yield (US10Y) to 4.75%, and the 30-year yield (US30Y) to 5%.

data.title: MultiPlan and J2 Health announces strategic agreement
data.description:
• MultiPlan (NYSE:MPLN) has announced an agreement with J2 Health, a cloud-based software solution that optimizes provider network performance.
  • This strategic agreement will support MultiPlan’s network optimization strategy, providing tailored solutions and resource efficiency that ultimately reduces healthcare costs for payors.
  • The partnership strengthens the distribution of solutions that unlock value and improve decision-making efficiency, supporting providers, payors, and the broader healthcare ecosystem.
  • “This agreement aims to unlock that value, which will ultimately drive better pricing outcomes and lower costs for our providers by increasing efficiency and reducing medical spend, allowing them to focus on higher quality, lower cost care.” said Travis Dalton, President and CEO.

data.title: KKR weighs $8.5B sale of U.K. recycling company Viridor - report
data.description:
KKR (NYSE:KKR) is speaking to potential advisors about a potential sale of U.K. waste management company Viridor Lid. -- among other options -- at a valuation as high as £7B ($8.5B), according to a media report dated Friday.

A sale process could be initiated later in 2025, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter, adding that considerations remain at an early stage and the asset could still be kept for longer if KKR (NYSE:KKR) so decides.

The potential sale of Viridor, which rakes in some £400M in annual EBITDA, comes amid heightened deal activity across the waste management industry as investors seek green assets and stable business models.

Earlier this week, for instance, Apollo Global Management (APO) and BC Partners agreed to buy a controlling stake in the environmental services unit of GFL Environment Inc. in a deal worth C$8B ($5.6B).

(KKR) shares slid 5.4% in Friday afternoon trading as the broader stock market suffered after a stronger-than-expected U.S. jobs report.

data.title: Handy Technologies settles with FTC for deceptive practices - updated
data.description:
Updated Jan 10 13:09ET to clarify that Handy Technologies, doing business as Angi Services, agreed to a settlement with the FTC, and includes statement from a company spokesperson.

Handy Technologies, doing business as Angi Services (NASDAQ:ANGI), agreed to a settlement with the U.S. Federal Trade Commission and ordered to make “substantial” changes to its business practices for misleading contractors on its platform regarding their earning potential, charging excessive fees for timely payments, and imposing fines on contractors for jobs that were cancelled by the requester.

The FTC complaint charges that Handy (NASDAQ:ANGI), advertised inflated hourly fees for gig jobs that “far outstrip any reasonable amount a worker can expect to be paid,” charged an additional fee to be paid as soon as the job is done, and other fees and fines that the company failed to adequately disclose.

“New York workers deserve to be paid what they are promised, when they are promised,” said New York Attorney General Letitia James, adding that “Apps like Handy’s offer New Yorkers flexible job opportunities, but they cannot be allowed to lure workers with lies and false promises.”

Under the terms of the settlement, Handy (NASDAQ:ANGI) would be required to pay $2.95M to refund harmed contractors and make changes to its platform to ensure contractors give clear consent to any fees that are charged by the company, as well as provide clear instructions how to avoid fines. The settlement would also require Handy (NASDAQ:ANGI) to back up any claims about how much contractors can potentially make.

"Though we were prepared to litigate, we chose to enter into an agreement with these parties to put this matter to rest and get back to putting our 100% focus on supporting our customers. None of the agencies' allegations were fair, and this settlement should in no way be construed as a validation of their allegations," a company spokesperson told Seeking Alpha.

This is the second time in less than two years that a company affiliated with Angi (NASDAQ:ANGI) agreed to settle with the FTC. In April 2023, the agency found that HomeAdvisor made false, misleading, or unsubstantiated claims about the quality and source of the leads it sells to service providers and for misleading service providers as to how much those projects would pay. HomeAdvisor agreed to pay $7.2M for redress and agreed from making any further false or misleading claims regarding its leads.

data.title: Money markets saw the biggest inflows since April ’20 - BofA
data.description:
Money markets funds saw the biggest inflow in cash since April 2020, up 11.4%, this week, according to BofA Securities data.

FICC inflows to money markets are up about 1.5% in assets under management to $208.5B year-to-date, and up 2.3% year-to-date.

 

 

 

Here are some of the top money market funds:

  • Vanguard Federal Money Market Fund (VMFXX)
  • Schwab Value Advantage Money Fund (SWVXX)
  • JPMorgan Prime Money Market Fund (VMVXX)
  • Invesco Government Money Market Fund (INAXX)
  • Fidelity Money Market Fund (SPRXX)
  • Vanguard Municipal Money Market Fund (VMSXX)
